diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the question.

options.label: A
options.text: $\frac{1}{12}$ the mass of an atom of the isotope carbon-12.

options.label: B
options.text: the amount of a substance that contains as many elementary entities as the number of atoms in $12\text{ g}$ of the isotope carbon-12.

options.label: C
options.text: the mass of one atom of the isotope carbon-12.

options.label: D
options.text: the amount of a substance that contains as many nuclei as the number of nuclei in $12\text{ g}$ of the isotope carbon-12.

vibe_explanation: The mole is a fundamental SI unit for the amount of substance. Its definition is historically based on the number of atoms found in exactly $12\text{ g}$ of carbon-12. Option A describes the unified atomic mass unit ($u$), not the mole. Option B correctly identifies that the mole refers to the 'amount of substance' and uses the standard reference of atoms in $12\text{ g}$ of carbon-12.
